items.headline: City Council reviews 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program
items.summary: Mountain View City Council held a study session on the Capital Improvement Program for fiscal year 2026-27, which outlines planned infrastructure and development projects. This is where residents can learn what physical improvements the city is prioritizing for the coming year.

items.headline: New tenant protections ordinance introduced for public hearing
items.summary: Sunnyvale City Council is considering changes to the Residential Tenant Protections Program through amendments to the municipal code. This ordinance could affect rental housing rules and tenant rights in the city.

items.headline: City releases Children and Youth Services Master Plan status
items.summary: The City Council reviewed progress on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan alongside annual reports on youth programs including the Safe Summer Initiative and youth intervention services. This update shows how the city is planning to support local youth services and summer programming.

items.headline: Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan moves forward
items.summary: Council received a status report on the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9, indicating ongoing planning for improvements to San José's library system and services. This suggests potential changes or upgrades to library locations and user experience in coming years.

items.headline: Downtown historic building could get 5,000 sq ft bonus
items.summary: City Council held a public hearing on elevating 405 Kipling Street from a Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation. This project affects downtown development capacity and preservation priorities.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed for San Antonio Road
items.summary: City Council reviewed a proposal to rezone and redevelop two properties on San Antonio Road into an eight-story building with 167 residential units (28 below-market rate) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. This represents significant residential growth in that corridor and requires rezoning from commercial to planned community zoning.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: A planned zoning hearing for Wednesday, April 8 was cancelled and rescheduled to April 22. A development project at 333 Franklin Street that residents were expecting to hear about will not be discussed on the original date and will be re-noticed when a new hearing is scheduled.

items.headline: City Council Approves Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the agenda. Residents interested in ongoing construction projects should monitor the city website for project scope and timeline details.
